content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the dashed center line visible, and the car appears well-positioned on the track heading into the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 103, target = (103 + 5) % 120 = 108
- Waypoint 108: x=1.1252, y=1.5186
- Car: x=1.1605, y=1.4196
- Bearing = atan2(1.5186 - 1.4196, 1.1252 - 1.1605) = atan2(0.0990, -0.0353) ≈ 109.6°
- Current heading: -50.47°
- Delta = 109.6° - (-50.47°) = 160.1° → normalize: 160.1° - 360° = -199.9°... 
- Re-normalize: 160.1° is already in range but very large positive. This means waypoint 108 is now behind us.
- Try waypoint 109: x=1.1570, y=1.3717
- Bearing = atan2(1.3717 - 1.4196, 1.1570 - 1.1605) = atan2(-0.0479, -0.0035) ≈ -94.2°
- Delta = -94.2° - (-50.47°) = -43.7° → right turn needed
- Try waypoint 110: x=1.1987, y=1.2281
- Bearing = atan2(1.2281 - 1.4196, 1.1987 - 1.1605) = atan2(-0.1915, 0.0382) ≈ -78.7°
- Delta = -78.7° - (-50.47°) = -28.2° → right turn needed

Delta ≈ -28° suggests moderate right steering needed as track curves right ahead.
